questions.question: What is the three-part formula for assertive communication?

options.text: You always... You never... You should...
I feel [feeling]... when [specific observation]... and I would appreciate [clear request]
I think... I want... I demand...
Please... Thank you... Sorry...

questions.explanation: The assertive communication formula is: 'I feel [your feeling] when [specific, non-judgmental observation] and I would appreciate [a clear, simple request].' This takes ownership of emotions without blaming.

questions.question: What is the difference between assertive and aggressive communication?

options.text: There is no difference
Assertive communication expresses needs respectfully while honoring both yourself and the other person
Assertive communication is louder and more forceful
Aggressive communication is always better in emergencies

questions.explanation: Assertiveness is not about being pushy or demanding. It is about expressing thoughts, feelings, and needs in an open, honest, and respectful way that honors both yourself and the other person.

questions.question: Why must the 'when' part of the formula use specific facts rather than judgments?

options.text: Because judgments are always wrong
Because facts keep the conversation constructive while judgments cause conflict and defensiveness
Because only facts are legally admissible
Because judgments take too long to explain

questions.explanation: 'When the dishes are left in the sink' is a fact. 'When you are being lazy' is a judgment that will cause conflict. Using specific, observable facts keeps the conversation constructive and prevents defensiveness.

questions.question: What should you consider when timing an assertive conversation?

options.text: Have the conversation in the heat of the moment when emotions are highest
Have the conversation when you are calm, not in the heat of the moment
Only have these conversations via text message
Wait until the problem resolves itself

questions.explanation: Timing is everything -- have assertive conversations when you are calm, not in the heat of the moment. The formula prevents getting sidetracked by blame or old arguments.

questions.question: How does using the assertive communication formula reduce anxiety?

options.text: It guarantees the other person will agree with you
It removes guesswork from difficult conversations and builds self-confidence over time
It prevents all conflict in relationships
It allows you to avoid difficult conversations

questions.explanation: Using a formula removes the guesswork from difficult conversations and gives a clear, respectful way to advocate for yourself. Over time, this builds self-confidence and reduces resentment, leading to less overall anxiety.
